sections.label: Section 513
sections.original: πῶς ; τίς δʼ ἀναγκάσει σε τήν γε σὴν κτανεῖν ;
sections.translation: How so ? who will compel you to slay your own ?

sections.label: Section 514
sections.original: ἅπας Ἀχαιῶν σύλλογος στρατεύματος .
sections.translation: The whole Achaean army here assembled .

sections.label: Section 515
sections.original: οὔκ , ἤν νιν εἰς Ἄργος γʼ ἀποστείλῃς πάλιν .
sections.translation: Not if you send her back to Argos .

sections.label: Section 516
sections.original: λάθοιμι τοῦτʼ ἄν . ἀλλʼ ἐκεῖνʼ οὐ λήσομεν .
sections.translation: I might do that unnoticed , but there will be another thing I cannot .

sections.label: Section 517
sections.original: τὸ ποῖον ; οὔτοι χρὴ λίαν ταρβεῖν ὄχλον .
sections.translation: What is that ? You must not fear the mob too much .

sections.label: Section 518
sections.original: Κάλχας ἐρεῖ μαντεύματʼ Ἀργείων στρατῷ .
sections.translation: Calchas will tell the Argive army his oracles .

sections.label: Section 519
sections.original: οὔκ , ἢν θάνῃ γε πρόσθε · τοῦτο δʼ εὐμαρές .
sections.translation: Not if he should die before that — an easy matter .

sections.label: Section 520
sections.original: τὸ μαντικὸν πᾶν σπέρμα φιλότιμον κακόν .
sections.translation: The whole tribe of seers is a curse with its ambition .

sections.label: Section 521
sections.original: κοὐδέν γʼ ἄχρηστον , οὐδὲ χρήσιμον παρόν .
sections.translation: Yes , and good for nothing and useless , when among us .

sections.label: Section 522
sections.original: ἐκεῖνο δʼ οὐ δέδοικας ὃ ἔμʼ ἐσέρχεται ;
sections.translation: Has the thought , which is rising in my mind , no terrors for you ?

sections.label: Section 523
sections.original: ὃν μὴ σὺ φράζεις , πῶς ὑπολάβοιμʼ ἂν λόγον ;
sections.translation: How can I understand your meaning , unless you declare it ?

sections.label: Section 524
sections.original: τὸ Σισύφειον σπέρμα πάντʼ οἶδεν τάδε .
sections.translation: The son of Sisyphus knows all .

sections.label: Section 525
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστʼ Ὀδυσσεὺς ὅ τι σὲ κἀμὲ πημανεῖ .
sections.translation: Odysseus cannot possibly hurt us .

sections.label: Section 526
sections.original: ποικίλος ἀεὶ πέφυκε τοῦ τʼ ὄχλου μέτα .
sections.translation: He was ever shifty by nature , siding with the mob .

sections.label: Section 527
sections.original: φιλοτιμίᾳ μὲν ἐνέχεται , δεινῷ κακῷ .
sections.translation: True , he is enslaved by the love of popularity , a fearful evil .

sections.label: Section 528
sections.original: οὐκ οὖν δοκεῖς νιν στάντʼ ἐν Ἀργείοις μέσοις
sections.translation: Don ’ t you think , then , he will arise among the Argives and tell them the oracles that Calchas delivered ,

sections.label: Section 530
sections.original: κἄμʼ ὡς ὑπέστην θῦμα , κᾆτʼ ἐψευδόμην ,
sections.translation: saying of me that I undertook to offer Artemis a victim , and after all am proving false ? Then , when he has carried the army away with him , he will bid the Argives slay us and sacrifice the girl ; and if I escape to Argos , they will come and destroy the place ,

sections.label: Section 535
sections.original: συναρπάσουσι καὶ κατασκάψουσι γῆν .
sections.translation: razing it to the ground , Cyclopean walls and all . That is my trouble . Woe is me ! to what perplexities the gods have brought me at this pass ! Take one precaution for me , Menelaus , as you go through the army , that Clytemnestra does not learn this ,

sections.label: Section 540
sections.original: μάθῃ , πρὶν Ἅιδῃ παῖδʼ ἐμὴν προσθῶ λαβών ,
sections.translation: till I have taken my child and devoted her to death , that my affliction may be attended with the fewest tears . Turning to the Chorus . And you , foreign women , keep silence . Exit Menelaus .

sections.label: Section 1.543
sections.original: — μάκαρες οἳ μετρίας θεοῦ
sections.translation: Happy are they who find the goddess come in moderate might , sharing with self-restraint

sections.label: Section 1.545
sections.original: σχον λέκτρων Ἀφροδίτας ,
sections.translation: in Aphrodite ’ s gift of marriage and enjoying calm and rest from frenzied passions , where the Love-god , golden-haired , stretches his charmed bow with twin arrows ,

sections.label: Section 1.550
sections.original: τὸ μὲν ἐπʼ εὐαίωνι πότμῳ ,
sections.translation: and one is aimed at happiness , the other at life ’ s confusion . O lady Cypris , queen of beauty ! far from my bridal bower I ban the last . Be mine delight in moderation

sections.label: Section 1.555
sections.original: χάρις , πόθοι δʼ ὅσιοι ,
sections.translation: and pure desires , and may I have a share in love , but shun excess !

sections.label: Section 1.558
sections.original: — διάφοροι δὲ φύσεις βροτῶν ,
sections.translation: Men ’ s natures vary , and their habits differ ,

sections.label: Section 1.560
sections.original: θῶς ἐσθλὸν σαφὲς αἰεί ·
sections.translation: but virtue is always manifest . Likewise the training that come of education conduces greatly to virtue ; for not only is modesty wisdom , but it has also the rare grace [*]

sections.label: Section 1.565
sections.original: χάριν ὑπὸ γνώμας ἐσορᾶν
sections.translation: of seeing by its better judgment what is right ; whereby a glory , ever young , is shed over life by reputation . A great thing it is to hunt virtue , for women when they love

sections.label: Section 1.570
sections.original: κρυπτάν , ἐν ἀνδράσι δʼ αὖ
sections.translation: secretly ; while in men , an inborn sense of order , shown in countless ways , [*] adds to a city ’ s greatness .

sections.label: Section 573
sections.original: — ἔμολες , ὦ Πάρις , ᾗτε σύ γε
sections.translation: You came , O Paris , to the place where you were reared to herd the cows

sections.label: Section 575
sections.original: Ἰδαίαις παρὰ μόσχοις ,
sections.translation: among the white heifers of Ida , piping in foreign strain and breathing on your reeds an echo of the Phrygian airs Olympus played . Full-uddered cows were browsing at the spot

sections.label: Section 580
sections.original: ὅτε σε κρίσις ἔμηνε θεᾶν ,
sections.translation: where [*] that verdict between goddesses was awaiting you — the cause of your going to Hellas to stand before the ivory palace , kindling love in Helen ’ s

sections.label: Section 585
sections.original: ἔρωτά τʼ ἔδωκας , ἔρωτι δʼ
sections.translation: entranced eyes and feeling its flutter in your own breast ; from which the fiend of strife brought Hellas with her spear and ships to the towers of Troy .

sections.label: Section 590
sections.original: ἰὼ ἰώ · μεγάλαι μεγάλων
sections.translation: Oh ! great is the bliss the great enjoy . Behold Iphigenia , the king ’ s child , my lady , and Clytemnestra , the daughter of Tyndareus ; how proud their lineage !

sections.label: Section 595
sections.original: ἐπί τʼ εὐμήκεις ἥκουσι τύχας .
sections.translation: how high their pinnacle of fortune ! These mighty ones , whom wealth attends , are very gods in the eyes of less favored folk .

sections.label: Section 598
sections.original: στῶμεν , Χαλκίδος ἔκγονα θρέμματα ,
sections.translation: Let us stand here , maidens of Chalcis , and lift the queen from her chariot

sections.label: Section 600
sections.original: ἄπο μὴ σφαλερῶς ἐπὶ γαῖαν ,
sections.translation: to the ground without stumbling , supporting her gently in our arms , with kind intent , that the renowned daughter of Agamemnon , just arrived , may feel no fear ; strangers ourselves , let us avoid anything that may disturb

sections.label: Section 605
sections.original: ταῖς Ἀργείαις
sections.translation: or frighten the strangers from Argos . [*]

sections.label: Section 607
sections.original: ὄρνιθα μὲν τόνδʼ αἴσιον ποιούμεθα ,
sections.translation: I take this as a lucky omen , your kindness and auspicious greeting , and have good hope that it is to a happy marriage

sections.label: Section 610
sections.original: πάρειμι νυμφαγωγός . ἀλλʼ ὀχημάτων
sections.translation: I conduct the bride . To attendants . Take from the chariot the dowry I am bringing for my daughter and convey it within with careful heed .

sections.label: Section 613
sections.original: σὺ δʼ , ὦ τέκνον μοι , λεῖπε πωλικοὺς ὄχους ,
sections.translation: My daughter , leave the horse-drawn chariot , planting your faltering footstep delicately . [*] To the Chorus .

sections.label: Section 615
sections.original: ὑμεῖς δέ , νεάνιδές , νιν ἀγκάλαις ἔπι
sections.translation: Young women , [*] take her in your arms and lift her from the chariot , and let one of you give me the support of her hand , that I may quit my seat in the carriage with fitting grace .

sections.label: Section 619
sections.original: αἳ δʼ ἐς τὸ πρόσθεν στῆτε πωλικῶν ζυγῶν ·
sections.translation: Some of you stand at the horses ’ heads ;

sections.label: Section 620
sections.original: φοβερὸν γὰρ ἀπαράμυθον ὄμμα πωλικόν ·
sections.translation: for the horse has a timid eye , easily frightened ; here , take this child Orestes , son of Agamemnon , baby as he still is .

sections.label: Section 623
sections.original: τέκνον , καθεύδεις πωλικῷ δαμεὶς ὄχῳ ;
sections.translation: What ! sleeping , little one , tired out by your ride in the chariot ? Awake to bless your sister ’ s wedding ; for you , my gallant boy ,

sections.label: Section 625
sections.original: ἀνδρὸς γὰρ ἀγαθοῦ κῆδος αὐτὸς ἐσθλὸς ὢν
sections.translation: shall get by this marriage a kinsman gallant as yourself , the Nereid ’ s godlike offspring . Come here to your mother , my daughter , Iphigenia , and seat yourself beside me , and stationed near show my happiness to these strangers ;

sections.label: Section 630
sections.original: καὶ — δεῦρο δὴ — πατέρα πρόσειπε σὸν φίλον .
sections.translation: yes , come here and welcome the father you love so dearly . [*]

sections.label: Section 631
sections.original: ὦ μῆτερ , ὑποδραμοῦσά σʼ · ὀργισθῇς δὲ μή ·
sections.translation: Do not be angry with me , mother , if I run from your side and throw myself on my father ’ s breast .

sections.label: Section 633
sections.original: ὦ σέβας ἐμοὶ μέγιστον , Ἀγαμέμνων ἄναξ ,
sections.translation: Hail ! my honored lord , king Agamemnon ! we have obeyed your commands and have come . [*]

sections.label: Section 635
sections.original: ἐγὼ δὲ βούλομαι τὰ σὰ στέρνʼ , ὦ πάτερ ,
sections.translation: O my father ! I long to outrun others and embrace you after this long while ; for I yearn to see your face ; do not be angry with me .

sections.label: Section 638
sections.original: ἀλλʼ , ὦ τέκνον , χρή · φιλοπάτωρ δʼ ἀεί ποτʼ εἶ
sections.translation: You may do so , daughter ; for of all the children I have borne , you have always loved your father best .

sections.label: Section 640
sections.original: ὦ πάτερ , ἐσεῖδόν σʼ ἀσμένη πολλῷ χρόνῳ .
sections.translation: I see you , father , joyfully after a long time .

sections.label: Section 641
sections.original: καὶ γὰρ πατὴρ σέ · τόδʼ ἴσον ὑπὲρ ἀμφοῖν λέγεις .
sections.translation: And I , your father , see you ; your words do equal duty for both of us .

sections.label: Section 642
sections.original: χαῖρʼ · εὖ δέ μʼ ἀγαγὼν πρὸς σʼ ἐποίησας , πάτερ .
sections.translation: All hail , father ! you did well in bringing me here to you .

sections.label: Section 643
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδʼ ὅπως φῶ τοῦτο καὶ μὴ φῶ , τέκνον .
sections.translation: I know not how I am to say yes or no to that , my child .

sections.label: Section 644
sections.original: ἔα ·
sections.translation: Ah ! how wildly you are looking , spite of your joy at seeing me .

sections.label: Section 645
sections.original: πόλλʼ ἀνδρὶ βασιλεῖ καὶ στρατηλάτῃ μέλει .
sections.translation: A man has many cares when he is king and general too .

sections.label: Section 646
sections.original: παρʼ ἐμοὶ γενοῦ νῦν , μὴ ʼπὶ φροντίδας τρέπου .
sections.translation: Be mine , all mine today ; do not turn to moody thoughts .

sections.label: Section 647
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἰμὶ παρὰ σοὶ νῦν ἅπας κοὐκ ἄλλοθι .
sections.translation: Why so I am , all yours today ; I have no other thought .

sections.label: Section 648
sections.original: μέθες νυν ὀφρὺν ὄμμα τʼ ἔκτεινον φίλον .
sections.translation: Then smooth your knitted brow , unbend and smile .

sections.label: Section 649
sections.original: ἰδού , γέγηθά σʼ ὡς γέγηθʼ ὁρῶν , τέκνον .
sections.translation: See ! my child , my joy at seeing you is even as it is .

sections.label: Section 650
sections.original: κἄπειτα λείβεις δάκρυʼ ἀπʼ ὀμμάτων σέθεν ;
sections.translation: And do you then have tears streaming from your eyes ?
